name: A167 DURHAM WESTERN BY PASS 45

surface_od_m.qualifier: as written: Ground level 67.40 m OD (sheet 1 header)

units: metres; typed Wimpey Geotech site-investigation log, transcribed verbatim. Depth = base of interval below ground level; OD Level column cross-checked (GL 67.40 minus depth = OD, all exact).

intervals.verbatim: MADE GROUND (brown very sandy very silty CLAY with much fine to coarse angular gravel sized fragments of sandstone and fragments of ash and coal)
Very loose brown clayey silty fine and medium SAND
Completely weathered brown silty fine and medium grained SANDSTONE
annotations: residual/weathered SANDSTONE recovered in cable-percussion drilling
base of cable percussion / start of rotary core drilling at 3.20m
borehole advanced by chiselling 3.10m-3.15m (1h) per Remarks

intervals.verbatim: Pale grey fine grained slightly weathered thinly convolute laminated silty SANDSTONE - strong and moderately strong. Closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures are occasionally pervasively discoloured orange and clay filled
annotations: OD base 63.30 (at 4.10m); lower contact is inclined 4.10m-4.50m, OD 62.90 printed at 4.50m

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained moderately to highly weathered thinly planar laminated sandy SILTSTONE - moderately weak. Very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Some moderately and completely weathered bands of sandstone towards top
annotations: thin weathered siltstone wedge between two inclined contacts; upper contact 4.10m-4.50m (OD 63.30 / 62.90 printed), base taken at core-run tick 5.20m which carries NO printed OD (approx OD 62.20)
base depth uncertain owing to inclined contact

intervals.verbatim: Widely banded orange and discoloured white fine to medium grained fresh to slightly weathered bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong and strong to very strong. Some beds with extremely closely spaced 5 degree siltstone laminae and occasional thin laminae and lenses of coal. Closely and medium occasionally widely spaced 5 degree to 25 degree bedding plane fractures and occasional subvertical fractures. Fractures usually dark or orangeish brown and black ironstained
annotations: occasional thin laminae and lenses of coal noted within sandstone (not a mineable seam)
spans sheet 1 to sheet 2 (See previous sheet)

intervals.verbatim: Greyish white fine grained slightly weathered thinly convolute laminated silty micaceous SANDSTONE - strong. Occasional thin laminae and lenses of coal. Beds of dark grey thinly ripple laminated sandy siltstone from 12.80m to 13.15m and 13.30m to 13.45m. Dark grey highly weathered highly fractured siltstone from 11.85m to 11.95m, 12.75m to 12.80m, 12.90m to 13.00m, 13.80m to 13.85m, 14.00m to 14.05m and 14.10m to 14.25m. Closely occasional close to medium spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Some 70 degree to subvertical dark brown ironstained fractures
annotations: contains thin interbedded sandy siltstone and highly weathered siltstone bands (see verbatim depths)

intervals.verbatim: Pale greyish white fine becoming fine to medium grained faintly thinly planar laminated micaceous SANDSTONE - strong. Occasional very thin carbonaceous laminae. Closely and medium spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Some 45 degree to 60 degree dark orangeish brown ironstained fractures. Bed of completely discoloured orange moderately to highly weathered weak sandstone from 16.60m to 16.75m
annotations: contains a completely discoloured/weathered weak sandstone bed 16.60m-16.75m

intervals.verbatim: Pale greyish white fine to medium and fine grained slightly weathered bedded micaceous SANDSTONE - strong. Many thin coal laminae throughout. Coal bands up to 5mm thick are convolute and are concentrated in beds of greyer fine grained sandstone with some thin siltstone bands and mudstone clasts. Medium occasionally closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures
annotations: many thin coal laminae and coal bands up to 5mm within the sandstone (not a mineable seam)

intervals.verbatim: Black highly fractured COAL - very weak
annotations: no seam name given in the original log
GWe: groundwater encountered at 19.80m (Remarks); 'GWe' marked in water-depth column at this level
spans sheet 2 to sheet 3 (See next sheet)

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained highly becoming moderately weathered bedded silty carbonaceous MUDSTONE - moderately weak to moderately strong. Very closely spaced irregular subhorizontal fractures. Abundant carbonaceous plant fossils

Pale greyish white fine grained slightly weathered thinly planar, convolute and ripple laminated silty micaceous SANDSTONE - strong, locally moderately strong. Occasional beds up to 0.20m thick of dark grey laminated siltstone with sandstone laminae. Occasional slumped bedding at top. Medium occasionally closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal to 10 degree bedding plane fractures
annotations: occasional dark grey laminated siltstone beds up to 0.20m thick
casing depth 3.20m, water depth 9.90m noted on this sheet

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained slightly weathered planar and slightly ripple laminated locally slightly fissile sandy SILTSTONE - strong to moderately strong. Bed of pale grey sandstone with extremely closely spaced rippled siltstone laminae from 28.40m to 28.75m. Highly weathered and highly fractured from 25.65m to 25.75m and highly fractured from 25.95m to 26.15m. Closely and close to medium spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures from 27.35m to 27.80m. Occasional subvertical fractures
annotations: contains a pale grey sandstone bed 28.40m-28.75m; localized highly weathered/fractured zones (see verbatim)

intervals.verbatim: Black fine grained slightly weathered thinly planar laminated slightly fissile carbonaceous MUDSTONE - moderately strong and moderately weak to moderately strong. Very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures. Common subvertical and occasional curved fractures. Some thin very strong brownish grey concretionary mudstone
annotations: thin very strong brownish grey concretionary mudstone bands
spans sheet 3 to sheet 4 (See previous sheet)

intervals.verbatim: Black highly fractured COAL - very weak
annotations: no seam name given in the original log

intervals.verbatim: Dark grey fine grained thinly convolute laminated sandy carbonaceous SILTSTONE - strong. Many bands 20-100mm thick of slumped sandstone. Some brown siltstone concretions. Many carbonaceous plant fossils. Closely and close to medium spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures
annotations: many slumped sandstone bands 20-100mm; brown siltstone concretions; many carbonaceous plant fossils
core run 100/64/(43); rotary run tick at 31.15m

intervals.verbatim: White fine grained fresh bedded SANDSTONE - very strong. Many short convolute siltstone lenses

Dark grey fine grained fresh thinly planar laminated SILTSTONE - strong. Many thin sandstone laminae. Closely and very closely spaced subhorizontal bedding plane fractures and occasional 60 degree fractures

notes: Typed Wimpey Geotech 'Borehole Record' (Fig. 50), Borehole 45, A167 Durham Western Bypass; 4 sheets. Boring: Cable Percussion to 3.20m, Rotary Core Drilling (Boyles BBS20 / 412 Core Barrel / Mist Flush) to 33.25m. Commenced 7/4/90. GL 67.40 m OD. Sheets 2-4 are continuation sheets with blank boring-method / ground-level header boxes (header data only on sheet 1). CLERK ARITHMETIC: every printed OD Level equals 67.40 minus the depth exactly across all 19 intervals - no sum slips. Two thin COAL beds, both 'Black highly fractured COAL - very weak' and both UNNAMED in the original (19.20-19.80m, 0.60m; 30.55-30.90m, 0.35m); additional coal occurs only as thin laminae/lenses within sandstones (intervals 7, 8, 10). No pencil marginalia / seam correlations present (modern typed log). ONLY notable ambiguity: the sandy-siltstone bed at interval 6 (approx 4.10-5.20m) lies between two INCLINED contacts - the upper contact is drawn sloping 4.10m (OD 63.30) to 4.50m (OD 62.90) with both OD ticks printed, and the base is taken at the core-run tick 5.20m which carries no printed OD; base depth is therefore 'probable'. Remarks (sheet 4): borehole advanced by chiselling 3.10m-3.15m (1h); groundwater encountered at 19.80m; full return of circulation mist during rotary drilling; borehole backfilled with bentonite/cement grout. Hand-added BGS reference 'NZ24SE/198' at top of every sheet; form title reads 'A167, DURHAM WESTERN BYPASS' (BGS name has the space as 'BY PASS').
